title: Stenoloma blumeanum
taxonomy: Stenoloma blumeanum is classified as a species of fern in the family Aspleniaceae. It belongs to the genus Stenoloma, which comprises a group of ferns commonly known as the "boulder ferns." This species is native to the tropical regions of Asia and is typically found growing on rocks and tree trunks. The classification of Stenoloma blumeanum is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its lance-shaped fronds and delicate, black-brown spores. Through its classification, botanists and researchers can better understand the evolutionary relationships and distribution of this species.

The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
